title: Fix rand_mode()/constraint_mode() crash when used as function arguments
body:
## Summary

Passing `rand_mode()` or `constraint_mode()` as a function/task argument causes an internal error. Per IEEE 1800-2017 §18.8/§18.9, these methods return int values and should be usable in any expression context, including function arguments.

## Current Behavior

- `check_mode("x", x.rand_mode(), 1)` crashes with `%Error: Internal Error: V3Begin.cpp:386: unlinked`
- Same crash for `constraint_mode()` as function argument
- Standalone calls (`x.rand_mode(0)`) and expression use (`if (x.rand_mode())`) work fine

## Expected Behavior

- `rand_mode()` and `constraint_mode()` return values should be usable as function/task arguments without error
- Elaboration completes, simulation runs correctly

## Source

- planvtech/PlanV_Verilator_Feature_Tests#26

---
PR is coming.
